statement: VERSION3.0 MAY NATIONAL DATA CLASSIFICATION POLICY [IAP-NAT-DCLS]
implementation_hint: Implement a comprehensive data classification system to ensure all national data is properly categorized and secured.

statement: Any reproduction of the present document either in part or full and irrespective of the means of reproduction; shall acknowledge NCSA as the source and owner of the “National Data Classification Policy”.
implementation_hint: Acknowledge NCSA as the source and owner of the 'National Data Classification Policy'.

statement: Any reproduction concerning this document with intent of commercialization shall seek a written authorization from the NCSA.
implementation_hint: Seek written authorization from NCSA before reproducing this document for commercial use.

statement: NCSA shall reserve the right to assess the functionality and applicability of all such reproductions developed for commercial intent.
implementation_hint: Ensure that all software developed for commercial use complies with NCSA's licensing policies.

statement: The authorization from NCSA shall not be construed as an endorsement of the developed reproduction and the developer shall in no way publicize or misinterpret this in any form of media or personal / social discussions.
implementation_hint: Ensure that the authorization from NCSA is treated as non-endorsing and do not publicize or misinterpret it.

statement: Abstract Review/Approval DateVersionReviewed/ApprovedDept./RoleName May 20233.0 National Cyber Governance and Assurance Affairs Director of National Cyber Governance and Assurance Affairs Revision History DateRevision DescriptionAuthor(s)Version January 2010 PublishedictQATAR1.0 February 2014PublishedMOTC2.0 May 2023PublishedNCSA – National Cyber Governance & Assurance Affairs3.0
implementation_hint: Ensure all abstract review and approval dates are up-to-date and accurate.

statement: In the event, a conflict arises between this document (specific provision or clauses) and the laws of Qatar, the latter (law), shall take precedence.
implementation_hint: Ensure that all legal documents comply with the laws of Qatar.

statement: Any such term (specific provision or clauses), to that extent shall be deemed omitted from this document, without affecting the remaining provisions of this document.
implementation_hint: Remove the specific provision or clauses from the document without altering the rest of the document.

statement: Amendments in that case shall then be required to ensure compliance with the relevant applicable laws of the State of Qatar.
implementation_hint: Ensure compliance with Qatar's laws through amendments.

statement: Therefore, the need for data management is the cornerstone that organizations must implement by having a comprehensive overview and establishing the foundations for managing data in a business environment.
implementation_hint: Establish a data management framework to ensure compliance with regulations.

statement: It cannot be hidden that without the use of a unified system and methodology for classifying data, all organizations will have different levels of asset protection, with no defined interpretations, which may hinder the business flow especially since data is mobile in nature.
implementation_hint: Implement a standardized data classification system to ensure uniform protection and clear interpretations.

statement: The National Information Assurance Standard is the complementary document to this document, which clarifies the main controls that organizations must take after they classify the data, which is updated according to the Procedures Fellowed.
implementation_hint: Ensure adherence to the National Information Assurance Standard for updated data classification controls.

statement: Data Classification Principles This policy is based on the following set of core principles that must be considered when classifying data: Principle 1: Understanding the nature of data Understanding data is the starting point in the methodology used to classify data.
implementation_hint: Ensure all data is clearly labeled and categorized based on its sensitivity.

statement: It is important to understand data as it may have a bearing on the way it is handled, stored, secured, processed, or exchanged.
implementation_hint: Implement data classification and labeling to ensure proper handling and storage.

statement: Therefore, the process of classifying data must consider data lifecycle.
implementation_hint: Implement a data classification system that tracks and categorizes data based on its lifecycle stages.

statement: Therefore, risk assessment is one of the basic principles that must be considered when classifying data, which determines the sensitivity and importance of this data for the work carried out by the organization.
implementation_hint: Implement data classification based on risk assessment to ensure compliance with organizational policies.

statement: The data classification process must achieve the most appropriate classification level which is as low as possible but as high as necessary, along with the necessary level of protection without exaggeration and without adding any administrative, financial, or technical burdens that may constitute an obstacle to the conduct of business within the organization.
implementation_hint: Ensure data is classified to the lowest appropriate level necessary, with the appropriate level of protection without unnecessary administrative, financial, or technical burdens.

statement: A high-level governance must be established across the organization with clearly defined roles and responsibilities for the different stakeholders within the organization.
implementation_hint: Establish clear policies and procedures for governance roles and responsibilities across the organization.

statement: The value of the data is not fixed, as its value may change according to the stage in which it is located, which is reflected in the degree of its classification.
implementation_hint: Automate the classification process to ensure timely updates and accurate categorization.

statement: In some stages, they are of high value and sometimes they may lose their value completely, which leads to the need to decommission data.
implementation_hint: Implement regular data review and evaluation to ensure data remains valuable and reduces the risk of data loss.

statement: For non-government organizations, some flexibility may be permitted in the choice of labels such as for C3 levels, “Confidential” could be used instead of “secret”, but it is necessary that the organization maintains one unified label scheme in its classification process.
implementation_hint: Ensure a consistent label scheme across all classifications.

statement: Administrative and technical tools that enable the use of labels must also be provided including color- coding of label.
implementation_hint: Ensure that administrative and technical tools are provided to enable the use of labels, including color-coding of labels.

statement: Sensitive Date if compromised could negatively affect operations Date for Internal Use Date that may be freely disclosed to the public Intended Audience: For your Eyes Only Intended Audience: Highly defined small set of users Intended Audience: Defined users, roles or groups based on specific business rules Intended Audience: Organization Users Intended Audience: Public Figure.
implementation_hint: Implement strict access controls and encryption for sensitive data.

statement: 2: Data Classification Labels Scheme However, the protection of the associated assets shall also be considered through their rating levels (high, medium, low).
implementation_hint: Implement a data classification scheme that includes rating levels for assets.

statement: Each organization shall create its own internal Data Classification policy that is committed to following the data classification approach, in line with legislations in the State of Qatar, such as Personal Data Privacy Protection Law (Law No.13 for 2016), and Right to Access Information Law (Law No.
implementation_hint: Develop and adhere to an internal Data Classification policy that complies with the Qatar Data Protection Law and the Right to Access Information Law.

statement: The selected controls must be subject to the state of the data, as it may be either data-in-transit, data-in-use, or data-at-rest.
implementation_hint: Ensure that all data is properly classified and secured according to its state (in transit, in use, or at rest).

statement: All users must be aware of the tasks and duties related to data management.
implementation_hint: Implement a training program for all users on the tasks and duties related to data management.

statement: Therefore, the organization must provide the required training to employees about the classification method used in the organization and follow up on compliance with it.
implementation_hint: Ensure employees are trained on the organization's classification method and regularly review compliance.

statement: The organization must provide technical solutions to facilitate the process of data classification, including data inventory, classification tools, adding tags, the method of sharing and processing it through its various stages in proportion to the required level of protection.
implementation_hint: Implement a data classification system with tools and tags to ensure data is handled according to its level of protection.

statement: The classification must be viewed in a balanced manner that achieves the required level of protection without overdoing it.
implementation_hint: Implement a balanced approach to security measures to ensure adequate protection without unnecessary complexity.

statement: These recommended classification labels shall not be used as default and it should be subject to apply the recommended data classification methodology 8.
implementation_hint: Ensure that the recommended classification labels are not used as default and follow the recommended data classification methodology 8.

statement: Data Consumer or User: is the person who deals with, uses and processes data according to the workflow entrusted to him, and he must be knowledgeable, sufficiently informed, and responsible for adhering to the optimal way to use and protect data according to the policies followed in the organization and to abide by the tasks entrusted to him during the stages of data management data and not expose it to potential risks.
implementation_hint: Ensure all data consumers are trained on the proper handling and protection of data.

statement: The policy defines five (5) levels of classification shall be used for government entities in Qatar.
implementation_hint: Implement a classification system for government entities in Qatar.

statement: Non- government entities shall use a minimum of four (4) levels of classification.
implementation_hint: Implement a minimum of four classification levels for non-government entities.

statement: In line with the classification levels, the organizations shall use a corresponding Data Classification Labels.
implementation_hint: Implement a system to automatically assign and update Data Classification Labels based on the classification levels.

statement: The default classification label for unclassified date shall be C1 – Internal.
implementation_hint: Ensure all unclassified data is properly labeled with the C1 – Internal classification.

statement: The data shall be secured and protected based on the security classification covering Confidentiality, Integrity, and Availability.
implementation_hint: Implement strong encryption and access controls to ensure data confidentiality and integrity.

statement: The data classification principles defined within this policy (Section 4) shall be used as the guiding force to arrive at the appropriate classification level for the data.
implementation_hint: Ensure all data is classified according to the defined principles.

statement: The organizations shall manage their data throughout the data life cycle and ensure that suitable processes are implemented to facilitate the same.
implementation_hint: Implement a comprehensive data management policy and ensure that all data handling processes are documented and followed.

statement: In line with this policy, each organization shall issue its own policy mandating the Data Classification Scheme provided in this policy.
implementation_hint: Ensure all organizations have their own policy mandating the Data Classification Scheme.

statement: The “Chief Data Officer” shall ensure that the Data Classification Program: a.
implementation_hint: Ensure that the Chief Data Officer is responsible for implementing and maintaining the Data Classification Program.

statement: The National Cyber Governance and Assurance affairs shall be the competent department for administrating this policy within the NCSA.
implementation_hint: Establish a dedicated Cyber Governance and Assurance department within the NCSA to oversee policy implementation.

statement: Any other deviations to this policy shall be communicated to the National Cyber Security Agency by the organization, through an official correspondence explaining the justifications and rational, along with a risk management plan identifying the risks, assessment of the risk, mitigating controls, and communication and acceptance of the risk by senior management.
implementation_hint: Communicate deviations to the National Cyber Security Agency via official correspondence, justifying actions, and implementing risk management plans.

statement: DATA REASSESSMENT The value of data may change from time to time based on various factors such as processing cycle, time, right of use, applicability to business, etc.
implementation_hint: Regularly review and update data policies and practices to ensure they remain relevant and effective.

statement: Based on this reassessment, corrective action must be taken on the controls applied.
implementation_hint: Review and update controls as necessary.

statement: Once data has served its purpose, it must be decommissioned and deleted from systems to end its life cycle.
implementation_hint: Implement a system or process to automatically delete data after its intended use has been fulfilled.

statement: Agencies should consult with their legal department and establish data retention policies within their organization.
implementation_hint: Consult legal department to establish data retention policies.

statement: All legal requirements, business requirements, contractual requirements and other should be considered when determining the retention period for data.
implementation_hint: Review and update retention periods based on legal, business, and contractual requirements.

statement: Legal requirements: You may have a legally binding reason to delete data from your systems.
implementation_hint: Ensure compliance with legal requirements for data deletion by implementing a clear and documented data retention policy.

statement: User requests: under data protection laws such as Qatar PDPPL and GDPR, users may request organizations to delete their personal data from their systems.
implementation_hint: Implement a system that allows users to request the deletion of their personal data.

statement: Takedown requests: the organization may knowingly or unknowingly have illegal, unlicensed or proprietary information on their systems that may constitute a breach of the law.
implementation_hint: Implement strict access controls and regularly review system logs to detect and remove unauthorized access attempts.

statement: If such information is identified, it must be removed with immediate effect.
implementation_hint: Implement strict data deletion procedures to ensure all identified sensitive information is removed immediately.

statement: Organizations must also consider whether it is necessary to obtain permission before releasing data and, if so, implement appropriate controls.
implementation_hint: Review data release policies and implement access controls as needed.

statement: Lastly, users must be educated about and use safe disposal methods depending on the classification of the data.
implementation_hint: Provide training sessions on safe data disposal methods.

statement: And although certain steps shall be implemented at the onset across the organization, when it comes to technical implementation of controls you may feel the need to break the technical implementation in defined sub scopes.
implementation_hint: Break down technical implementation into manageable sub-scopes for better control and easier management.

statement: Step Step 11:: Management commitment: Management must formally introduce the data classification program in their organization.
implementation_hint: Ensure that management formally introduces the data classification program in the organization through a documented communication and action plan.

statement: Step Step 22: : Management must appoint a person as ‹Chief Data Officer› who has the appropriate skills and business understanding to lead and manage the program.
implementation_hint: Designate a Chief Data Officer with relevant skills and business acumen to lead the data management program.

statement: Step Step 33: : Management must allocate the necessary resources (funds, staff) to the program.
implementation_hint: Ensure that the budget and staffing for the program are clearly defined and regularly reviewed.

statement: Therefore, organizations should conduct a Business Impact Analysis (BIA) to identify the critical processes/functions in the organization.
implementation_hint: Perform a Business Impact Analysis to identify critical processes.

statement: These critical processes/functions should be prioritized over other unimportant business processes.
implementation_hint: Implement a prioritization system for critical processes to ensure they are given the necessary resources and attention.

statement: If the Agency has an existing method for assessment of business impact, this lowercase to may be used instead of the one provided in this Appendix.
implementation_hint: Replace the existing method for assessing business impact with the one provided in this Appendix.

statement: The following steps should be undertaken to rate a processes criticality: 1.
implementation_hint: Evaluate process effectiveness and identify key performance indicators.
